# A7 — A small, safe compiler for the agent era

A7 lowers .a7 source to Zig, then to a native binary via the host Zig 0.16 toolchain.
No source recursion. Iterative compiler internals. Deterministic exit codes (0/2-8).

Site:    https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/
Full:    https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/llms-full.txt
Source:  https://github.com/Airbus5717/a7-py

## Pages

- Getting Started — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/learn/start

  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/getting-started.md

  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/why.md

  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/examples.md

### REFERENCE

- Language Reference — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/ref/language
  The implemented A7 language surface. Anything you can see in examples/ compiles end-to-end. Anything described here works in the current …
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/language.md
- CLI — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/ref/cli
  The A7 compiler ships one entrypoint: a7. It's installed by uv sync as a console script. Use it via uv run a7 ... or import the Python AP…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/cli.md
- API — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/ref/api
  The compiler is a normal Python package — a7/ — that you can import and drive programmatically. The CLI (a7.cli:main) is the canonical en…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/api.md
- Standard Library — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/ref/stdlib
  A7's standard library is small by design. Each module is a single virtual import resolved by the compiler — no source file is loaded from…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/stdlib.md
- Features — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/ref/features
  This page draws the line between supported features (compile + run end-to-end) and reserved ones (syntax may parse, but the backend rejec…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/features.md

### COMPILER

- Compiler Internals — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/internals
  The A7 compiler is a single Python package, a7/, structured as a pipeline of independent stages. There is no runtime. Every stage runs ah…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/compiler.md
- Pipeline — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/pipeline
  Per-stage detail for the A7 compile pipeline. Each stage takes the previous stage's output, runs an iterative traversal, and hands off to…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/pipeline.md
- Safety — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/safety
  A7's safety model is a single compile-time pass that proves or rejects risky operations before the backend emits Zig. There is no runtime…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/safety.md
- Testing — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/testing
  A7's tests live in test/ at the repository root. The suite is the contract between the compiler and the language; the docs are downstream…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/testing.md
- Implementation Status — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/status
  What works, what's incomplete, and what's deferred. This page is the canonical roadmap. Keep docs/CHANGELOG.md short and release-facing; …
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/status.md
- Release — https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/compiler/release
  How an A7 release ships. Tags drive builds; builds drive artifacts; artifacts attach to GitHub releases. There is no package-registry pub…
  Source: https://code5717.github.io/a7-py/docs/release.md
